<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_873

<a id="sec-6-27"></a>
## 6.27 Vital Product Data (VPD) | 重要产品数据 (VPD)

table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Vital Product Data (VPD) is the information that uniquely defines items such as the hardware, software, and microcode elements of a system. The VPD provides the system with information on various FRUs (Field Replaceable Unit) including Part Number, Serial Number, and other detailed information. VPD also provides a mechanism for storing information such as performance and failure data on the device being monitored. The objective, from a system point of view, is to collect this information by reading it from the hardware, software, and microcode components.

Support of VPD within add-in cards is optional depending on the manufacturer. Though support of VPD is optional, add-in card manufacturers are encouraged to provide VPD due to its inherent benefits for the add-in card, system manufacturers, and for Plug and Play.

The mechanism for accessing VPD is documented in § Section 7.9.18.

VPD for PCI Express is unchanged from the definition in the [PCI-3.0]. That definition, in turn, was based on earlier versions of the [PCI] as well as the [PLUG-PLAY-ISA-1.0a].

Vital Product Data is made up of Small and Large Resource Data Types.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

重要产品数据 (Vital Product Data, VPD) 是唯一定义系统硬件、软件和微代码元素等信息的数据。VPD 向系统提供有关各种 FRU (现场可更换单元, Field Replaceable Unit) 的信息,包括部件号 (Part Number)、序列号 (Serial Number) 和其他详细信息。VPD 还提供一种机制,用于在被监控设备上存储性能和故障数据等信息。从系统的角度来看,目标是通过从硬件、软件和微代码组件中读取这些信息来收集它们。

插卡 (add-in card) 中对 VPD 的支持是可选的,具体取决于制造商。尽管 VPD 支持是可选的,但鉴于 VPD 对插卡、系统制造商以及即插即用 (Plug and Play) 的固有优势,鼓励插卡制造商提供 VPD。

访问 VPD 的机制在 § Section 7.9.18 中记录。

PCI Express 的 VPD 与 [PCI-3.0] 中的定义一致。该定义反过来又基于早期版本的 [PCI] 以及 [PLUG-PLAY-ISA-1.0a]。

重要产品数据由 Small (小型) 和 Large (大型) Resource Data Type 组成。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

**Table 6-19. Small Resource Data Type Tag Bit Definitions | 表 6-19. 小型资源数据类型标签位定义**

| Offset | Field Name | Value/Description |
|--------|------------|-------------------|
| Byte 0 | Value = 0xxx xyyyb | (Tag value) |
| Bit 7 | Small Resource Type | 0b |
| Bits 6:3 | Small Item Name | xxxx |
| Bits 2:0 | Length in bytes | yy |
| Bytes 1 to n | Actual information | (Data) |

**Table 6-20. Large Resource Data Type Tag Bit Definitions | 表 6-20. 大型资源数据类型标签位定义**

| Offset | Field Name | Value/Description |
|--------|------------|-------------------|
| Byte 0 | Value = 1xxx xxxxb | (Tag value) |
| Bit 7 | Large Resource Type | 1b |
| Bits 6:0 | Large Item Name | xxxxxxx |
| Byte 1 | Length in bytes of data items bits[7:0] (lsb) | (Length LSB) |
| Byte 2 | Length in bytes of data items bits[15:8] (msb) | (Length MSB) |
| Bytes 3 to n | Actual data items | (Data) |

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-6-27-vpd-intro"></a>
## 6.27 (续) VPD 标签与示例 | VPD Tags and Example


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The first VPD tag is the Identifier String (02h) and provides the product name of the device.

One VPD-R (10h) tag is used as a header for the read-only keywords. The VPD-R list (including tag and length) must checksum to zero. Attempts to write the read-only data will be executed as a no-op.

One VPD-W (11h) tag is used as a header for the read-write keywords. The storage component containing the read/write data is a non-volatile device that will retain the data when powered off.

The last tag must be the End Tag (0Fh).

A small example of the resource data type tags used in a typical VPD is shown in § Table 6-21.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

第一个 VPD 标签是 Identifier String (标识字符串, 02h),它提供设备的产品名称。

使用一个 VPD-R (10h) 标签作为只读关键字的头部。VPD-R 列表(包括 tag 和 length)必须校验和为零。尝试写入只读数据将作为 no-op(空操作)执行。

使用一个 VPD-W (11h) 标签作为读写关键字的头部。包含读/写数据的存储组件是非易失性设备,断电后将保留数据。

最后一个标签必须是 End Tag (结束标签, 0Fh)。

典型 VPD 中所用资源数据类型标签的小型示例如 § Table 6-21 所示。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


**Table 6-21. Resource Data Type Flags for a Typical VPD | 表 6-21. 典型 VPD 的资源数据类型标志**

| Tag | Type | Description |
|-----|------|-------------|
| TAG 02h | Large Resource Data Type | Identifier String (标识字符串) |
| TAG 10h | Large Resource Data Type | VPD-R list containing one or more VPD keywords (包含一个或多个 VPD 关键字的 VPD-R 列表) |
| TAG 11h | Large Resource Data Type | VPD-W list containing one or more VPD keywords (包含一个或多个 VPD 关键字的 VPD-W 列表) |
| TAG 0Fh | Small Resource Data Type | End Tag (结束标签) |

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_874

<a id="sec-6-27-1"></a>
## 6.27.1 VPD Format | VPD 格式

table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Information fields within a VPD resource type consist of a three-byte header followed by some amount of data (see § Figure 6-34). The three-byte header contains a two-byte keyword and a one-byte length. A keyword is a two-character (ASCII) mnemonic that uniquely identifies the information in the field. The last byte of the header is binary and represents the length value (in bytes) of the data that follows.

```
7  6  5  4  3  2  1  0  +0   Keyword
7  6  5  4  3  2  1  0  +1   Keyword
7  6  5  4  3  2  1  0  +2   Length (N)
7  6  5  4  3  2  1  0  +3   Data (bytes 3 to N+2, figure shows N = 6)
                          Byte 0 →
                          Byte 4 →
                          Byte 8 →
```

Figure 6-34 VPD Format

VPD keywords are listed in two categories: read-only fields and read/write fields. Unless otherwise noted, keyword data fields are provided as ASCII characters. Use of ASCII allows keyword data to be moved across different enterprise computer systems without translation difficulty.

An example of the "add-in card serial number" VPD item is as follows:

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

VPD 资源类型内的信息字段由一个三字节头部和随后的若干数据组成(参见 § Figure 6-34)。三字节头部包含一个两字节的 keyword (关键字) 和一个一字节的 length (长度)。关键字是一个两字符(ASCII)的助记符,用于唯一标识字段中的信息。头部的最后一个字节是二进制,表示后续数据的长度值(以字节为单位)。

```
7  6  5  4  3  2  1  0  +0   Keyword (关键字)
7  6  5  4  3  2  1  0  +1   Keyword (关键字)
7  6  5  4  3  2  1  0  +2   Length (长度, N)
7  6  5  4  3  2  1  0  +3   Data (数据, 字节 3 至 N+2,图中 N = 6)
                          Byte 0 →
                          Byte 4 →
                          Byte 8 →
```

Figure 6-34 VPD 格式

VPD 关键字分为两类:只读字段和读/写字段。除非另有说明,关键字数据字段以 ASCII 字符提供。ASCII 的使用允许关键字数据可在不同企业计算机系统之间传输而无需翻译。

"插卡序列号" (add-in card serial number) VPD 条目示例如下:

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 6-34.** VPD Format
> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0874_1_tight.png" width="700">

**Table 6-22. Example of Add-in Card Serial Number | 表 6-22. 插卡序列号示例**

| Byte | Value | Field | Description |
|------|-------|-------|-------------|
| Byte 0 | 53h "S" | Keyword: SN (第一个字符) | Keyword 第一个字符 'S' |
| Byte 1 | 4Eh "N" | Keyword: SN (第二个字符) | Keyword 第二个字符 'N' |
| Byte 3 | 08h | Length: 8 | 长度: 8 |
| Byte 4 | 30h "0" | Data: "00000194" | 数据字符 '0' |
| Byte 5 | 30h "0" | Data: "00000194" | 数据字符 '0' |
| Byte 6 | 30h "0" | Data: "00000194" | 数据字符 '0' |
| Byte 7 | 30h "0" | Data: "00000194" | 数据字符 '0' |
| Byte 8 | 30h "0" | Data: "00000194" | 数据字符 '0' |
| Byte 9 | 31h "1" | Data: "00000194" | 数据字符 '1' |
| Byte 10 | 39h "9" | Data: "00000194" | 数据字符 '9' |
| Byte 11 | 34h "4" | Data: "00000194" | 数据字符 '4' |

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_875

<a id="sec-6-27-2"></a>
## 6.27.2 VPD Definitions | VPD 定义

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

This section describes the current VPD large and small resource data tags plus the VPD keywords. This list may be enhanced at any time. Companies wishing to define a new keyword should contact the PCISIG. All unspecified values are reserved for SIG assignment.

VPD is contained in four types of Large and Small Resource Data Tags. The following tags and VPD keyword fields may be provided in PCI devices.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

本节介绍当前的 VPD 大型和小 Resource Data Tag 以及 VPD 关键字。此列表可随时增强。希望定义新关键字的公司应联系 PCISIG。所有未指定的值均保留供 SIG 分配。

VPD 包含在四种类型的大型和小型 Resource Data Tag 中。以下标签和 VPD 关键字字段可在 PCI 设备中提供。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

**Table 6-23. VPD Large and Small Resource Data Tags | 表 6-23. VPD 大型与小型资源数据类型标签**

| Tag | Type | Description |
|-----|------|-------------|
| String Tag (02h) | Large resource type Identifier | This tag is the first item in the VPD storage component. It contains the name of the add-in card in alphanumeric characters. (此标签是 VPD 存储组件中的第一项。它以字母数字字符包含插卡的名称。) |
| VPD-R Tag (10h) | Large resource type VPD-R Tag | This tag contains the read only VPD keywords for an add-in card. (此标签包含插卡的只读 VPD 关键字。) |
| VPD-W Tag (11h) | Large resource type VPD-W Tag | This tag contains the read/write VPD keywords for an add-in card. (此标签包含插卡的读/写 VPD 关键字。) |
| End Tag (0Fh) | Small resource type End Tag | This tag identifies the end of VPD in the storage component. (此标签标识存储组件中 VPD 的结束。) |

**Table 6-24. VPD Read-Only Fields | 表 6-24. VPD 只读字段**

| Keyword | Name | Description |
|---------|------|-------------|
| PN | Add-in Card Part Number | This keyword is provided as an extension to the Device ID (or Subsystem ID) in the Configuration Space header in § Figure 6-34. (此关键字作为 Configuration Space header 中 Device ID (或 Subsystem ID) 的扩展提供,参见 § Figure 6-34。) |
| EC | Engineering Change Level of the Add-in Card | The characters are alphanumeric and represent the engineering change level for this add-in card. (字符为字母数字,表示此插卡的工程变更级别。) |
| FG | Fabric Geography | Reserved for legacy use by [PICMG] specifications. (保留供 [PICMG] 规范遗留使用。) |
| LC | Location | Reserved for legacy use by [PICMG] specifications. (保留供 [PICMG] 规范遗留使用。) |
| MN | Manufacture ID | This keyword is provided as an extension to the Vendor ID (or Subsystem Vendor ID) in the Configuration Space header in § Figure 6-34. This allows vendors the flexibility to identify an additional level of detail pertaining to the sourcing of this device. (此关键字作为 Configuration Space header 中 Vendor ID (或 Subsystem Vendor ID) 的扩展提供,参见 § Figure 6-34。这允许供应商灵活地识别与该设备采购相关的额外详细信息级别。) |
| PG | PCI Geography | Reserved for legacy use by [PICMG] specifications. (保留供 [PICMG] 规范遗留使用。) |
| SN | Serial Number | The characters are alphanumeric and represent the unique add-in card Serial Number. (字符为字母数字,表示唯一的插卡序列号。) |

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---


---

<a id="sec-6-27-2-1"></a>
### 6.27.2.1 VPD Large and Small Resource Data Tags | VPD 大资源和小资源数据标签


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 6-24 VPD Read-Only Fields**

| Keyword | Name | Description |
|---------|------|-------------|
| TR | Thermal Reporting | This keyword provides a standard interface for reporting four fields: AFI Level, MaxTherm, DTherm, and MaxAmbient. The data area for this field is four bytes long. This data is encoded as a 4-byte binary value in little endian order (byte 0 contains bits 7:0). This value contains the four fields as follows: AFI Level bits [3:0], MaxTherm bits [7:4], DTherm bits [11:8], and MaxAmbient bits [19:12] are placed in bits 19:0. Bits 31:20 are Reserved and must be set to 000h. Field description is provided within the [CEM]. This keyword is intended to be used only in designs based on that form factor specification. Note that due to the character nature of the VPD encoding mechanism, this binary value is permitted to start on any byte boundary within the VPD. |
| Vx | Vendor Specific | This is a vendor specific item and the characters are alphanumeric. The second character (x) of the keyword can be 0 through 9 or A through Z. |
| CP | Extended Capability | This field allows a new capability to be identified in the VPD area. Since dynamic control/status cannot be placed in VPD, the data for this field identifies where, in the device's memory or I/O address space, the control/status registers for the capability can be found. Location of the control/status registers is identified by providing the index (a value between 0 and 5) of the Base Address register that defines the address range that contains the registers, and the offset within that Base Address register range where the control/status registers reside. The data area for this field is four bytes long. The first byte contains the ID of the extended capability. The second byte contains the index (zero based) of the Base Address register used. The next two bytes contain the offset (in little-endian order) within that address range where the control/status registers defined for that capability reside. |
| RV | Checksum and Reserved | The first byte of this item is a checksum byte. The checksum is correct if the sum of all bytes in VPD (from VPD address 0 up to and including this byte) is zero. The remainder of this item is reserved space (as needed) to identify the last byte of read-only space. The read-write area does not have a checksum. This field is required. |
| FF | Form Factor | This keyword indicates a string that identifies the form factor and version associated with this add-in-card. Values are a lower case string. The string consists of a list of one or more elements separated by colons (":"). The first element is a sequence of lowercase strings separated by periods that identifies the specification(s) associated with the form factor. The first element string is the reserved domain name associated with the authority defining that form factor (i.e., like those used in [DNS] records), followed by one or more strings that identify a particular form factor, followed by a Version Number for that form factor specification. Any characters in the form factor name other than "a" to "z", "0" to "9", "*", "?", and "-" are dropped (e.g., M.2 becomes m2). The character "*" represents a wild card (arbitrary characters, including "."). The value "-" is used to indicate no form factor. The value "?" or the absence of the FF keyword is used to indicate that the form factor is unknown. Subsequent elements are optional and contain attributes describing variations of that form factor (e.g., size, connector, keying, …). These elements are defined by the indicated form-factor specifiation and consist of either an "option" string or a "key=value" string. Valid characters in the "option" or "key" portion are "a" to "z", "0" to "9", "*", "?", and "-". The "value" portion may contain any character other than the colon ":". The order of attributes is not significant. Attributes are not permitted when the first element is "-" or "?" (no form factor or unknown form factor). Examples are:<br>• com.pcisig.cem.4.0<br>• com.pcisig.cem.*<br>• com.pcisig.m2.1.0:2280<br>• com.pcisig.m2.1.0:size=2280:key=M<br>• com.pcisig.m2.1.0:bga<br>• org.snia.sff-ta-1001.1.0.2<br><br>A given add-in card is permitted to claim conformance to multiple form factor specifications. This is indicated by using the wild card character "*" or by using multiple FF fields (which in turn could use the wild card character). When the form factor of a slot does not match some FF keyword of the add-in card in that slot, this indicates the presence of one or more "Carrier Cards" to convert power and sideband signals of the slot to those of the add-in card. The mechanism(s) used to identify a particular Carrier Card and to describe how it operates are outside the scope of this specification. |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**表 6-24 VPD 只读字段**

| 关键字 | 名称 | 描述 |
|--------|------|------|
| TR | 热报告 (Thermal Reporting) | 此关键字提供一个标准接口用于上报四个字段：AFI Level、MaxTherm、DTherm 和 MaxAmbient。该字段的数据区为 4 字节长。数据以小端序 (little endian) 编码为 4 字节二进制值 (byte 0 包含 bit 7:0)。该值按如下方式承载四个字段：AFI Level 占用 bit [3:0]，MaxTherm 占用 bit [7:4]，DTherm 占用 bit [11:8]，MaxAmbient 占用 bit [19:12]，它们一起放置在 bit 19:0 之内。Bit 31:20 为保留位 (Reserved)，必须设置为 000h。字段描述见 [CEM]。此关键字仅用于基于该外形规格 (Form Factor) 规范的设计中。需要注意的是，由于 VPD 编码机制的字符特性，该二进制值允许从 VPD 内的任意字节边界开始。 |
| Vx | 厂商特定 (Vendor Specific) | 这是一个厂商特定项，字符为字母数字形式。关键字的第二个字符 (x) 可以是 0–9 或 A–Z。 |
| CP | 扩展能力 (Extended Capability) | 该字段用于在 VPD 区域中标识一个新的能力 (Capability)。由于动态控制/状态不能放在 VPD 中，此字段的数据指明：在设备内存或 I/O 地址空间中，该能力的控制/状态寄存器所在位置。控制/状态寄存器的位置通过提供基址寄存器 (Base Address Register) 的索引 (0–5 之间的值) 来标识，索引对应包含寄存器的地址范围，以及在该基址寄存器范围内的偏移 (offset) 来指明控制/状态寄存器的具体位置。该字段的数据区为 4 字节长。第一个字节包含扩展能力的 ID。第二个字节包含所用基址寄存器的索引 (从 0 开始)。接下来两个字节以小端序表示该地址范围内的偏移 (offset)，即该能力定义的控制/状态寄存器所在的地址。 |
| RV | 校验和与保留 (Checksum and Reserved) | 该项的第一个字节为校验和字节。当 VPD 中所有字节 (从 VPD 地址 0 到包括此字节) 之和为零时，校验和正确。该项的其余部分为保留空间 (按需)，用于标识只读空间的最后一个字节。读/写区域没有校验和。此字段是必需的。 |
| FF | 外形规格 (Form Factor) | 此关键字表示一个字符串，用于标识与该插卡 (add-in-card) 关联的外形规格和版本。值为小写字符串。该字符串由一个或多个元素组成，元素之间以冒号 (":") 分隔。第一个元素是由句点分隔的小写字符串序列，用于标识与该外形规格关联的规范。第一个元素字符串是为该外形规格定义机构保留的域名 (即与 [DNS] 记录所用类似)，后跟一个或多个用于标识特定外形规格的字符串，再后跟该外形规格规范的版本号 (Version Number)。外形规格名称中除 "a"–"z"、"0"–"9"、"*"、"?"、"-" 以外的任何字符都将被丢弃 (例如 M.2 变成 m2)。字符 "*" 表示通配符 (任意字符，包括 ".")。值 "-" 用于表示无外形规格。值 "?" 或 FF 关键字的缺失用于表示外形规格未知。后续元素是可选的，包含描述该外形规格变体的属性 (例如尺寸、连接器、键位等)。这些元素由所引用的外形规格规范定义，由 "option" 字符串或 "key=value" 字符串组成。"option" 或 "key" 部分的合法字符为 "a"–"z"、"0"–"9"、"*"、"?"、"-"。"value" 部分可包含除冒号 ":" 以外的任何字符。属性的顺序无关紧要。当第一个元素为 "-" 或 "?" (无外形规格或未知外形规格) 时不允许使用属性。示例如下：<br>• com.pcisig.cem.4.0<br>• com.pcisig.cem.*<br>• com.pcisig.m2.1.0:2280<br>• com.pcisig.m2.1.0:size=2280:key=M<br>• com.pcisig.m2.1.0:bga<br>• org.snia.sff-ta-1001.1.0.2<br><br>允许一张插卡 (add-in card) 声明符合多个外形规格规范。这通过使用通配符 "*" 或使用多个 FF 字段 (这些字段本身也可以使用通配符) 来表示。当插槽的外形规格与该插槽中插卡的某个 FF 关键字不匹配时，这表示存在一个或多个 "转接卡 (Carrier Cards)"，用于将插槽的电源和边带 (Sideband) 信号转换为该插卡所对应的信号。用于识别特定转接卡并描述其工作方式的机制不在本规范范围内。 |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<a id="sec-6-27-2-2"></a>
### 6.27.2.2 Read-Only Fields | 只读字段

table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 6-25 VPD Read/Write Fields**

| Keyword | Name | Description |
|---------|------|-------------|
| Vx | Vendor Specific | This is a vendor specific item and the characters are alphanumeric. The second character (x) of the keyword can be 0 through 9 or A through Z. |
| Yx | System Specific | This is a system specific item and the characters are alphanumeric. The second character (x) of the keyword can be 0 through 9 or B through Z. |
| YA | Asset Tag Identifier | This is a system specific item and the characters are alphanumeric. This keyword contains the system asset identifier provided by the system owner. |
| RW | Remaining Read/Write Area | This descriptor is used to identify the unused portion of the read/write space. The product vendor initializes this parameter based on the size of the read/write space or the space remaining following the Vx VPD items. One or more of the Vx, Yx, and RW items are required. |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**表 6-25 VPD 读/写字段**

| 关键字 | 名称 | 描述 |
|--------|------|------|
| Vx | 厂商特定 (Vendor Specific) | 这是一个厂商特定项，字符为字母数字形式。关键字的第二个字符 (x) 可以是 0–9 或 A–Z。 |
| Yx | 系统特定 (System Specific) | 这是一个系统特定项，字符为字母数字形式。关键字的第二个字符 (x) 可以是 0–9 或 B–Z。 |
| YA | 资产标签标识符 (Asset Tag Identifier) | 这是一个系统特定项，字符为字母数字形式。此关键字包含系统所有者提供的系统资产标识符。 |
| RW | 剩余读/写区域 (Remaining Read/Write Area) | 此描述符用于标识读/写空间中未使用的部分。产品厂商根据读/写空间的大小或 Vx VPD 项之后剩余的空间初始化此参数。Vx、Yx 和 RW 项中的一项或多项是必需的。 |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_878

<a id="sec-6-28"></a>
## 6.28 Native PCIe Enclosure Management | 原生 PCIe 机箱管理

table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

NPEM is an optional PCIe Extended Capability that provides mechanisms for enclosure management. This mechanism is designed to provide management for enclosures containing PCIe SSDs that is consistent with the established capabilities in the storage ecosystem.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

NPEM (Native PCIe Enclosure Management) 是一种可选的 PCIe 扩展能力 (Extended Capability)，提供机箱管理机制。该机制旨在为包含 PCIe SSD 的机箱提供与存储生态系统中既有能力一致的管理功能。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

This section defines the architectural aspects of the mechanism. The NPEM extended capability is defined in Section 7.9.19.

An enclosure is any platform, box, rack, or set of boxes that contain one or more PCIe SSDs. The NPEM capability provides storage related enclosure control (e.g., status LED control) for a PCIe SSD. The NPEM capability may reside in a Downstream port, or an Endpoint (i.e., the PCIe SSD). Figure 6-35 shows an example configuration with a single Downstream Port containing the NPEM capability and vendor specific logic to control the associated LEDs.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

本节定义该机制的架构层面。NPEM 扩展能力在第 7.9.19 节中定义。

机箱 (enclosure) 是包含一个或多个 PCIe SSD 的任何平台、机箱、机架或一组机箱。NPEM 能力为 PCIe SSD 提供与存储相关的机箱控制 (例如状态 LED 控制)。NPEM 能力可以驻留于下游端口 (Downstream Port) 或端点 (Endpoint，即 PCIe SSD) 中。图 6-35 给出了一个示例配置：单个下游端口 (Downstream Port) 中包含 NPEM 能力以及用于控制相关 LED 的厂商特定逻辑。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 6-35.** Example NPEM Configuration using a Downstream Port
> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0879_1_tight.png" width="700">

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Figure 6-36 shows an example configuration with the NPEM capability located in the Upstream Port (in this case, the SSD function).

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

图 6-36 给出了一个示例配置：NPEM 能力位于上游端口 (Upstream Port) 中 (本例中即 SSD Function)。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 6-36.** Example NPEM Configuration using an Upstream Port
> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0880_1_tight.png" width="700">


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Software issues an NPEM command by writing to the NPEM Control register to change the indications associated with an SSD. NPEM Command is a single write to the NPEM Control register that changes the state of zero or more bits. NPEM indicates a successful completion to software using the command completed mechanism. Figure 6-37 shows the overall flow.

This specification defines the software interface provided by the NPEM capability. The Port to enclosure interface, enclosure, enclosure to LED interface, number of LEDs per SSD, and associated LED blink patterns are all outside the scope of this specification.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

软件通过对 NPEM 控制寄存器 (NPEM Control register) 写入来发出 NPEM 命令，以更改与 SSD 关联的指示。NPEM 命令 (NPEM Command) 是对 NPEM 控制寄存器的单次写入，用于更改零个或多个位的状态。NPEM 通过命令完成机制 (command completed mechanism) 向软件指示成功完成。图 6-37 显示了整体流程。

本规范定义了 NPEM 能力所提供的软件接口。端口到机箱的接口 (Port to enclosure interface)、机箱、机箱到 LED 的接口 (enclosure to LED interface)、每个 SSD 的 LED 数量以及相关的 LED 闪烁模式均不在本规范范围内。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


> **Figure 6-37.** NPEM Command Flow
> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0881_1.png" width="700">

table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

NPEM provides a mechanism for system software to issue a reset to the LED control element within the enclosure by means of the NPEM Reset mechanism, which is independent of the PCIe Link itself. The NPEM command completed mechanism also applies to NPEM Reset.

Storage system admin or software controls the indications for various device states through the NPEM capability.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

NPEM 提供了一种机制，使系统软件能够通过 NPEM 复位 (NPEM Reset) 机制对机箱内的 LED 控制单元发起复位，该机制独立于 PCIe 链路 (Link) 本身。NPEM 命令完成机制同样适用于 NPEM 复位。

存储系统管理员或软件通过 NPEM 能力控制各种设备状态的指示。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_879

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_880

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_881

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_882

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:** NPEM STATES

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Table 6-27 shows an example of NPEM states and a possible meaning that some enclosures may assign to the architected NPEM states.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

表 6-27 给出 NPEM 状态示例，以及某些机箱可能为各架构化 NPEM 状态赋予的可能含义。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

**Table 6-27 NPEM States**

| NPEM State | Actor | Definition |
|------------|-------|------------|
| OK | System Admin or Storage Software | OK state may mean the drive is functioning normally. This state may implicitly mean that an SSD is present, powered on, and working normally as seen by the software. A more granular indication of drive not physically present or present but not powered up are both outside the scope of this specification. |
| Locate | System Admin | Locate state may mean the specific drive is being identified by an admin. |
| Fail | Storage Software | Fail state may mean the drive is not functioning properly |
| Rebuild | Storage Software | Rebuild state may mean this drive is part of a multi-drive storage volume/array that is rebuilding or reconstructing data from redundancy on to this specific drive. |
| PFA | Storage Software | PFA stands for Predicted Failure Analysis. This state may mean the drive is still functioning normally but predicted to fail soon. |
| Hot Spare | Storage Software | Hot Spare state may mean this drive is marked to be automatically used as a replacement for a failed drive and contents of the failed drive may be rebuilt on this drive. |
| In A Critical Array | Storage Software | In A Critical Array state may mean the drive is part of a multi-drive storage array and that array is degraded. |
| In A Failed Array | Storage Software | NPEM In A Failed Array state may mean the drive is part of a multi-drive storage array and that array is failed. |
| Invalid Device Type | Storage Software | Invalid Device Type state may mean the drive is not the right type for the connector (e.g., An enclosure supports SAS and NVMe drives and this drive state indicates that a SAS drive is plugged into an NVMe slot). |
| Disabled | Storage Software | Disabled state may mean the drive in this slot is disabled. A removal of this drive from the slot may be safe. The power from this slot may be removed. |

**表 6-27 NPEM 状态**

| NPEM 状态 | 行为主体 (Actor) | 定义 |
|------------|-------|------------|
| OK | 系统管理员或存储软件 | OK 状态可表示驱动器工作正常。此状态可隐含地表示软件所见的 SSD 已存在、已上电并正常工作。关于驱动器物理上不存在或存在但未上电的更细粒度指示不在本规范范围内。 |
| Locate (定位) | 系统管理员 | Locate 状态可表示特定驱动器正被管理员识别。 |
| Fail (故障) | 存储软件 | Fail 状态可表示驱动器工作不正常。 |
| Rebuild (重建) | 存储软件 | Rebuild 状态可表示该驱动器是多驱动器存储卷/阵列的一部分，正在将冗余数据重建或重构到该特定驱动器上。 |
| PFA (预测故障分析) | 存储软件 | PFA 代表 Predicted Failure Analysis (预测故障分析)。此状态可表示驱动器仍正常工作但预测很快会发生故障。 |
| Hot Spare (热备) | 存储软件 | Hot Spare 状态可表示该驱动器被标记为自动替代故障驱动器的备用盘，故障驱动器的内容可能会被重建到该驱动器上。 |
| In A Critical Array (处于临界阵列) | 存储软件 | In A Critical Array 状态可表示该驱动器是多驱动器存储阵列的一部分，且该阵列已降级。 |
| In A Failed Array (处于故障阵列) | 存储软件 | NPEM In A Failed Array 状态可表示该驱动器是多驱动器存储阵列的一部分，且该阵列已故障。 |
| Invalid Device Type (无效设备类型) | 存储软件 | Invalid Device Type 状态可表示驱动器与连接器类型不匹配 (例如机箱同时支持 SAS 和 NVMe 驱动器，而此驱动器状态表示 SAS 驱动器被插入到了 NVMe 插槽中)。 |
| Disabled (禁用) | 存储软件 | Disabled 状态可表示该插槽中的驱动器已被禁用。从插槽中移除该驱动器可能是安全的。该插槽的电源可被移除。 |

---

>>> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:** 软件轮询 NPEM 命令完成 (SOFTWARE POLLING OF NPEM COMMAND COMPLETED)


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Different NPEM implementations may vary widely in how long they take to complete NPEM commands, from instantaneous to tens of ms. To avoid or minimize software polling overheads, it is recommended that software implement one or both of the following optimizations.

Instead of software writing a command and then immediately polling for completion, it is recommended that software reverse this order. When ready to write a new command, software first polls for completion of the previous command, and then writes the new command. This enables overlapped operation, often completely hiding the time it takes hardware to execute an NPEM command. To enable this polling model, software must initialize the hardware following a reset by writing a no-op command in order to have hardware generate the first NPEM command completion.

For the case where an NPEM command has not completed when software polls the bit, it is recommended that software not continuously "spin" on polling the bit, but rather poll under interrupt at a reduced rate; for example at 10 ms intervals.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

不同的 NPEM 实现完成 NPEM 命令所需的时间差异很大，从瞬时到几十毫秒不等。为避免或最小化软件轮询开销，建议软件实现以下一项或两项优化。

建议颠倒软件的操作顺序：不是先写命令再立即轮询完成，而是当准备好写入新命令时，软件先轮询上一条命令的完成状态，然后写入新命令。这使得操作可以重叠进行，通常可以完全隐藏硬件执行 NPEM 命令所需的时间。为启用此轮询模式，软件必须在复位后通过写入一条 no-op (空操作) 命令来初始化硬件，以便让硬件产生首次 NPEM 命令完成。

对于软件轮询该位时 NPEM 命令尚未完成的情况，建议软件不要连续"自旋 (spin)"轮询该位，而应以较低的速率 (例如每 10 ms 一次) 在中断中进行轮询。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_883

<a id="sec-6-29"></a>
## 6.29 Conventional PCI Advanced Features Operation | 传统 PCI 高级特性操作

table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

For Conventional PCI devices integrated into a Root Complex, the Conventional PCI Advanced Features Capability (AF) provides mechanisms for using advanced features originally developed for PCI Express.

- The Function Level Reset (INITIATE_FLR) mechanism enables software to quiesce and reset hardware with Function-level granularity.

  FLR applies on a per Function basis. Only the targeted Function is affected by the FLR operation.
- The Transactions Pending (TP) mechanism is used to indicate that the Function has issued one or more non-posted transactions (including Delayed Transactions) which have not been completed.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

对于集成在根复合体 (Root Complex) 中的传统 PCI 设备，传统 PCI 高级特性能力 (Conventional PCI Advanced Features Capability, AF) 提供最初为 PCI Express 开发的若干高级特性的使用机制。

- Function 级复位 (Function Level Reset, INITIATE_FLR) 机制使软件能够以 Function 级粒度静默 (quiesce) 并复位硬件。

  FLR 按 Function 粒度生效。仅有目标的 Function 受 FLR 操作影响。
- 事务挂起 (Transactions Pending, TP) 机制用于指示该 Function 已发出一个或多个尚未完成的 Non-Posted 事务 (包括 Delayed Transactions)。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

The FLR and TP mechanisms defined here are strictly for Conventional PCI devices integrated into a Root Complex where the implementation permits non-posted transactions for a given Conventional PCI Function to complete even if the value of the Bus Master Enable bit in its Command Register is 0b. Implementations that do not meet this requirement must not implement the FLR and TP mechanisms.

FLR modifies the Function state as follows:

Function registers and Function-specific state machines must be set to their initialization values as specified in this document, except for the following bits, which must not be modified: Fast Back-to-Back Transactions Enable, Cache Line Size, Latency Timer, Interrupt Line, PME_En, PME_Status.

Note that the controls that enable the Function to initiate bus transactions are cleared, including the Bus Master Enable bit in the Command Register, the MSI Enable bit in the MSI Capability Structure, and the like, effectively causing the Function to become quiescent.

After an FLR has been initiated, the Function must complete the FLR within 100 ms. If software initiates an FLR when the Transactions Pending bit is 1b, then software must not initialize the Function until allowing adequate time to achieve reasonable certainty that any outstanding transactions will have completed. The Transactions Pending bit must be clear upon completion of the FLR.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

此处定义的 FLR 与 TP 机制严格限于集成在根复合体中的传统 PCI 设备，且其实现允许给定传统 PCI Function 的 Non-Posted 事务即使在 Command Register 中的 Bus Master Enable 位为 0b 时仍能完成。不满足此要求的实现不得实现 FLR 和 TP 机制。

FLR 按以下方式修改 Function 状态：

Function 寄存器和 Function 特定状态机必须设置为本规范规定的初始值，但以下位不得被修改：Fast Back-to-Back Transactions Enable、Cache Line Size、Latency Timer、Interrupt Line、PME_En、PME_Status。

注意，使 Function 能够发起总线事务的控制位会被清除，包括 Command Register 中的 Bus Master Enable 位、MSI 能力结构 (MSI Capability Structure) 中的 MSI Enable 位等，从而有效地使 Function 进入静默 (quiescent) 状态。

FLR 启动后，Function 必须在 100 ms 内完成 FLR。如果软件在 Transactions Pending 位为 1b 时启动 FLR，则软件必须在等待足够时间以合理确信任何未完成事务均将完成之后，才能对 Function 进行初始化。FLR 完成时，Transactions Pending 位必须清零。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_884

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:** AVOIDING ISSUES WITH PENDING TRANSACTIONS


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

FLR modifies Function state not described by this specification (in addition to state that is described by this specification), and so the following criteria must be applied using Function-specific knowledge to evaluate the Function's behavior in response to an FLR:

- The Function must not give the appearance of an initialized adapter with an active host on any external interfaces controlled by that Function. The steps needed to terminate activity on external interfaces are outside of the scope of this specification.
  - For example, a network adapter must not respond to queries that would require adapter initialization by the host system or interaction with an active host system, but is permitted to perform actions that it is designed to perform without requiring host initialization or interaction. If the network adapter includes multiple Functions that operate on the same external network interface, this rule affects only those aspects associated with the particular Function reset by FLR.
- The Function must not retain within itself software readable state that potentially includes secret information associated with any preceding use of the Function. Main host memory assigned to the Function must not be modified by the Function.
  - For example, a Function with internal memory readable directly or indirectly by host software must clear or randomize that memory.
- The Function must return to a state such that normal configuration of the Function's PCI interface will cause it to be useable by drivers normally associated with the Function.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

FLR 会修改本规范未描述的 Function 状态 (以及本规范已描述的状态之外的状态)，因此必须结合 Function 特定知识应用以下准则来评估 Function 对 FLR 的响应行为：

- Function 在其所控制的任何外部接口上，不得表现得像一个已初始化、且具有活动主机的适配器。终止外部接口活动所需的步骤不在本规范范围内。
  - 例如，网络适配器不得响应那些需要主机系统进行适配器初始化或与活动主机系统交互的查询，但允许执行其设计为可在无需主机初始化或交互的情况下执行的动作。如果网络适配器包含多个在同一外部网络接口上运作的 Function，则此规则仅影响与被 FLR 复位的特定 Function 相关的部分。
- Function 不得在其内部保留可能包含与 Function 此前使用相关的秘密信息的软件可读状态。分配给该 Function 的主宿主机内存不得被该 Function 修改。
  - 例如，具有可由主机软件直接或间接读取的内部存储器的 Function 必须清除或随机化该存储器。
- Function 必须返回到这样的状态：对 Function 的 PCI 接口进行正常配置即可使与该 Function 关联的驱动程序正常使用它。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

When an FLR is initiated, the targeted Function must behave as follows:

- The Function must complete normally the configuration write that initiated the FLR operation and then initiate the FLR.
- While an FLR is in progress:
  - The Function must not respond to any request on the bus (i.e., requests targeting the Function will Master Abort).

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

当 FLR 启动时，目标的 Function 必须表现为：

- Function 必须正常完成启动 FLR 操作的配置写入，然后再启动 FLR。
- 在 FLR 进行期间：
  - Function 不得响应总线上的任何请求 (即针对该 Function 的请求将出现 Master Abort)。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The Transactions Pending (TP) bit indicates that the Function has issued one or more non-posted transactions which have not been completed. This field may be used by software to determine when a Function has become quiescent.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

事务挂起位 (Transactions Pending, TP) 指示该 Function 已发出一个或多个尚未完成的 Non-Posted 事务。软件可使用此字段判断 Function 何时已变为静默 (quiescent)。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

>>> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:** AVOIDING ISSUES WITH PENDING TRANSACTIONS


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

An FLR causes a Function to lose track of any pending (outstanding non-posted) transactions. Depending upon the specific implementation of the RC-integrated PCI Function, if software issues an FLR while there are pending transactions, there is a possibility for data corruption as described in the "Avoiding Data Corruption From Stale Completions" Implementation Note.

To avoid potential issues with Root Complex implementations where Stale Completions are possible or a Discard Timer is present, it is recommended that software use an algorithm similar to the following:

1. Software that's performing the FLR synchronizes with other software that might potentially access the Function directly, and ensures that such accesses will not occur during this algorithm.
2. Software clears the entire Command register, disabling the Function from mastering any new transactions.
3. Software polls the Transactions Pending bit in the AF Status Register either until it's clear or until it's been long enough to achieve reasonable certainty that any remaining outstanding Transactions will never complete. On many systems, the Transactions Pending bit will usually clear within a few milliseconds, so software might choose to poll during this initial period using a tight software loop. On rare cases when the Transactions Pending bit doesn't clear by this time, software will need to poll for a longer system-specific period (potentially seconds), so software might choose to conduct this polling using a timer-based interrupt polling mechanism.
4. Software initiates the FLR.
5. Software waits 100 ms.
6. Software reconfigures the Function and enables it for normal operation.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

FLR 会导致 Function 失去对任何挂起 (outstanding non-posted) 事务的跟踪。根据 RC 集成 PCI Function 的具体实现，如果软件在仍有挂起事务时启动 FLR，则可能发生数据损坏，如实现说明 "Avoiding Data Corruption From Stale Completions" 所述。

为避免在可能出现陈旧完成 (Stale Completions) 或存在 Discard Timer 的根复合体实现中可能出现的问题，建议软件使用类似以下的算法：

1. 执行 FLR 的软件与其他可能直接访问该 Function 的软件进行同步，并确保在该算法执行期间不会发生此类访问。
2. 软件清除整个 Command Register，禁用该 Function 发起任何新事务的能力。
3. 软件轮询 AF Status Register 中的 Transactions Pending 位，直到该位清零或已等待足够长时间以合理确信任何剩余未完成事务都不可能完成。在许多系统中，Transactions Pending 位通常会在数毫秒内清零，因此软件可在此初始阶段使用紧凑的软件循环进行轮询。在少数情况下，若 Transactions Pending 位在此期间未清零，软件将需要轮询更长的系统特定时长 (可能为秒级)，因此软件可选择使用基于定时器的中断轮询机制来进行此轮询。
4. 软件启动 FLR。
5. 软件等待 100 ms。
6. 软件重新配置该 Function 并将其使能为正常工作。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<a id="sec-6-30"></a>
## 6.30 Data Object Exchange (DOE) | 数据对象交换 (DOE)

table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Data Object Exchange (DOE) is an optional mechanism for system firmware/software to perform data object exchanges with a Function or RCRB. Software discovers DOE support via the Data Object Exchange (DOE) Extended Capability structure. Because DOE depends on Configuration Requests it is not usable for peer-to-peer operations directly between Functions, although system software can provide a mechanism to relay data objects between Functions if such capabilities are desired. When DOE is implemented in an RCRB, it is permitted to block peer-to-peer operation via implementation specific means.

DOE is a prerequisite Extended Capability for a Function to support in-band access by system firmware/software using Configuration Requests to Component Measurement and Authentication (CMA). CMA in turn builds on [SPDM].

It is permitted to implement DOE in any type of Function, and in an RCRB. It is permitted to implement DOE more than once in a single Function or RCRB.

DOE uses the terms "type" and "feature" with specific meanings. A data object type indicates a group of one or more data objects, and can be used to determine how to further construct or parse a specific data object. Every data object includes an indication of its type. Data object types are defined by particular vendors and distinguished by Vendor ID. It is permitted for features/types defined by one vendor to require the use of features/types defined by another vendor.

A feature is a capability of some sort that uses data objects. A feature can be associated with one or more data object types. Each specific feature defines the mechanism(s) used by software to discover support for the feature, for example by means of a capabilities bit in Configuration Space, or implied by the presence of an associated Extended Capability structure. A feature using data objects must specify the scope of the specific features in relation to the Function(s) implementing that feature via DOE, for example if only the Function itself is associated with the feature, or if Function 0 of a Multi-Function Device represents the Device as a whole, etc.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

数据对象交换 (Data Object Exchange, DOE) 是一种可选机制，允许系统固件/软件与 Function 或 RCRB 进行数据对象交换。软件通过数据对象交换 (DOE) 扩展能力结构发现 DOE 支持。由于 DOE 依赖于配置请求 (Configuration Requests)，它不能直接用于 Function 之间的对等 (peer-to-peer) 操作；不过，系统软件可以提供在 Function 之间中继数据对象的机制 (如果需要此类能力)。当 DOE 在 RCRB 中实现时，允许通过实现特定手段阻止对等操作。

DOE 是 Function 通过配置请求支持组件测量与认证 (Component Measurement and Authentication, CMA) 的带内 (in-band) 访问的先决扩展能力。CMA 又基于 [SPDM]。

允许在任何类型的 Function 以及 RCRB 中实现 DOE。允许在单个 Function 或 RCRB 中多次实现 DOE。

DOE 以特定含义使用术语 "类型 (type)" 和 "特性 (feature)"。数据对象类型 (data object type) 指示一个或多个数据对象的分组，并可用于确定如何进一步构造或解析特定数据对象。每个数据对象都包含其类型的指示。数据对象类型由特定厂商定义并以 Vendor ID 区分。允许由一个厂商定义的 feature/type 要求使用另一厂商定义的 feature/type。

feature 是某种使用数据对象的能力 (capability)。一个 feature 可与一个或多个数据对象类型关联。每个具体的 feature 定义软件用于发现对该 feature 支持的机制，例如通过配置空间 (Configuration Space) 中的能力位 (capabilities bit)，或由关联的扩展能力结构的存在隐含指示。使用数据对象的 feature 必须规定该 feature 在通过 DOE 实现该 feature 的 Function 方面的作用范围，例如：仅该 Function 自身与该 feature 关联，或者多功能设备 (Multi-Function Device) 的 Function 0 代表整个设备等。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

It is permitted, but not recommended, for a feature using data object exchanges to require that a Function implement a unique instance of DOE for that specific feature, and/or to allow sharing of a DOE instance to only a specific set of features using data object exchange, and/or to allow a Function to implement multiple instances of DOE supporting the specific feature.

It is permitted to use DOE when a Function is in non-D0 states, although it is permitted for a specific data object feature to restrict operation in non-D0 states.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

允许但不推荐使用数据对象交换的 feature 要求 Function 为该特定 feature 实现唯一的 DOE 实例，以及/或者仅允许一组特定的使用数据对象交换的 feature 共享某个 DOE 实例，以及/或者允许 Function 实现多个支持该特定 feature 的 DOE 实例。

允许在 Function 处于非 D0 状态时使用 DOE，但允许特定的数据对象 feature 限制其在非 D0 状态下的操作。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_885

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_886

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_887

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:** SUPPORTING MULTIPLE HARDWARE/SOFTWARE BINDINGS


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

When multiple features are supported by a device, or when a given feature can be used by more than one software entity at a time, it is typically necessary to coordinate the use of the hardware/software interface between entities implementing the multiple features. SPDM Version 1.2.0 introduced the SPDM connection model, addressing some important needs, but not itself addressing the hardware/software interface problems arising when multiple software entities need to maintain different contexts and/or configurations. DOE provides two basic mechanisms for addressing this type of issue.

One option is that specific data object features may require the use of dedicated instances of DOE, particularly to allow system software to assign ownership of a specific DOE instance with the software entity using the specific data object feature(s) associated with that particular instance. For example, data object features A and B may perform different tasks, and so by instantiating dedicated instances of DOE for each, system software avoids the need to implement ownership control and arbitration mechanisms between A and B.

When this is done, if the underlying hardware uses shared resources to implement A and B, then the hardware may require the ability to maintain separate contexts for each data object feature, because system software will allow the two features to be operated at the same time.

Alternatively, for CMA/SPDM and related use cases, the optional Connection ID mechanism can eliminate the need for multiple DOE instances. Typically, the Connection ID mechanism provides much better scaling, and it is recommended that the Connection ID mechanism be used where applicable, instead of requiring the use of dedicated DOE instances. Figure 6-38 illustrates a "stack diagram" where six applications are all using CMA/SPDM over DOE in three groups.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

当设备支持多个 feature，或给定 feature 可被多个软件实体同时使用时，通常需要协调实现多个 feature 的实体之间对硬件/软件接口的使用。SPDM 1.2.0 版本引入了 SPDM 连接模型 (SPDM connection model)，解决了一些重要需求，但并未解决当多个软件实体需要维护不同上下文和/或配置时所产生的硬件/软件接口问题。DOE 提供两种基本机制来解决此类问题。

一种选择是：特定数据对象 feature 可能要求使用专用的 DOE 实例，特别是为了使系统软件能够将特定 DOE 实例的所有权分配给使用与该实例关联的特定数据对象 feature 的软件实体。例如，数据对象 feature A 和 B 可能执行不同任务，因此通过分别为其实例化专用的 DOE 实例，系统软件可避免在 A 和 B 之间实现所有权控制和仲裁机制。

当采用此方式时，如果底层硬件使用共享资源来实现 A 和 B，则硬件可能需要具备为每个数据对象 feature 维护单独上下文的能力，因为系统软件将允许这两个 feature 同时运行。

另一种选择是：对于 CMA/SPDM 及相关用例，可选的 Connection ID 机制可消除对多个 DOE 实例的需求。通常 Connection ID 机制提供更好的可扩展性，因此建议在适用场景下使用 Connection ID 机制，而不是要求使用专用 DOE 实例。图 6-38 给出了一个"栈式图 (stack diagram)"：六个应用程序分为三组，均通过 DOE 使用 CMA/SPDM。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


> **Figure 6-38.** Stack Diagram Illustration of Multiple Sessions and Connections
> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0887_1_tight.png" width="700">

table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

In each case, two distinct SPDM uses are shown, A and B, and within each group, the SPDM configuration must be the same, but each group may have distinct requirements from the others, e.g., different hashing algorithms. The SPDM Driver and PCI Driver software components must coordinate the establishment of each connection, including the assignment of a Connection ID, and, at run time, must manage the transfer of data objects across the DOE interface itself to ensure that multiple software entities cannot attempt to access the DOE Mailbox registers during the transfer of a single data object. However, context is maintained in the device for each session. Figure 6-39 illustrates at a high level how these elements relate to each other.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

在每种情况下，都展示了两种不同的 SPDM 用法 A 和 B；在每个组内，SPDM 配置必须相同，但各组之间可能有不同的需求，例如使用不同的哈希算法。SPDM 驱动 (SPDM Driver) 和 PCI 驱动 (PCI Driver) 软件组件必须协调每个连接的建立，包括 Connection ID 的分配，并且在运行时必须管理跨 DOE 接口本身的数据对象传输，以确保在单个数据对象的传输过程中多个软件实体不能同时尝试访问 DOE Mailbox 寄存器。但是，设备会为每个 session 维护上下文。图 6-39 概要性地展示了这些元素之间的关系。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 6-39.** Example Showing Relationships of Software and Hardware Elements
> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0888_1_tight.png" width="700">

---

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_888

<a id="sec-6-30-1"></a>
### 6.30.1 Data Objects and Features | 数据对象与特性

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Data objects must consist of 2 DW to 256K DW, as shown in Figure 6-40.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

数据对象必须由 2 DW 到 256K DW 组成，如图 6-40 所示。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 6-40.** DOE Data Object Format
> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0889_1_tight.png" width="700">

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The first DW of a data object must be formatted as defined in Table 6-28 and illustrated in Figure 6-41.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

数据对象的第一个 DW 必须按表 6-28 所定义并如图 6-41 所示的格式组织。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 6-41.** DOE Data Object Header 1
> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0889_2_tight.png" width="700">

**Table 6-28 DOE Data Object Header 1**

| Bit Location | Description |
|--------------|-------------|
| 15:0 | Vendor ID - PCI-SIG Vendor ID of the entity that defined the type of data object. |
| 23:16 | Data Object Type – The type of data object. |

**表 6-28 DOE 数据对象头 1**

| 位位置 (Bit Location) | 描述 (Description) |
|--------------|-------------|
| 15:0 | Vendor ID — 定义此数据对象类型的实体的 PCI-SIG Vendor ID。 |
| 23:16 | Data Object Type — 数据对象的类型。 |

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The Second DW of a data object must be formatted as defined in Table 6-29 and illustrated in Figure 6-42.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

数据对象的第二个 DW 必须按表 6-29 所定义并如图 6-42 所示的格式组织。

> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0889_3.png" width="700">

---

<<<PAGE_BREAK>>> page_889

**Table 6-29 DOE Data Object Header 2**

| Bit Location | Description |
|--------------|-------------|
| 17:0 | Length – Length of the data object being transferred in number of DWs including the 2 DWs of the Data Object Header, encoded such that a value of 0 0002h indicates 2 DWs, and a value of 0 0000h indicates 2^18 DWs. A value of 0 0001h is not allowed. |
| 29:18 | Connection ID – For data object types using Connection ID, this field contains the Connection ID. For all other data object types, this field is Reserved. |

**表 6-29 DOE 数据对象头 2**

| 位位置 (Bit Location) | 描述 (Description) |
|--------------|-------------|
| 17:0 | Length — 所传输数据对象的长度，以 DW 为单位，包括数据对象头的 2 DW。编码方式为：值 0 0002h 表示 2 DW，值 0 0000h 表示 2^18 DW。值 0 0001h 不允许。 |
| 29:18 | Connection ID — 对于使用 Connection ID 的数据对象类型，此字段包含 Connection ID。对于所有其他数据对象类型，此字段为保留 (Reserved)。 |


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Each data object is uniquely identified by the Vendor ID of the vendor publishing the data object definition and a Data Object Type value assigned by that vendor (see Table 6-28). See Table 6-33 for a list of data objects defined by PCI-SIG. Data objects of a specific type are permitted to vary in size and composition.

Unless a data object type definition specifies a different requirement, the following rules apply:

- If the number of DW transferred does not match the Length indicated in DOE Data Object Header 2 for a data object, then the entire data object must be silently discarded.
- If the Length indicated in DOE Data Object Header 2 is shorter than expected for a specific data object, then the data object must be silently discarded.
- If the Length indicated in DOE Data Object Header 2 is greater than expected for a specific data object, then the portion of the data object up to the expected length must be processed normally and the remainder of the data object must be silently discarded.
- If the data object type is not supported, then that data object must be silently discarded.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

每个数据对象由发布该数据对象定义的厂商的 Vendor ID 和该厂商分配的 Data Object Type 值唯一标识 (见表 6-28)。表 6-33 列出了由 PCI-SIG 定义的数据对象。特定类型的数据对象在大小和组成上可以有所不同。

除非数据对象类型定义规定了不同的要求，否则适用以下规则：

- 如果传输的 DW 数与该数据对象 DOE 数据对象头 2 中指示的 Length 不匹配，则整个数据对象必须被静默丢弃。
- 如果 DOE 数据对象头 2 中指示的 Length 短于特定数据对象所期望的长度，则该数据对象必须被静默丢弃。
- 如果 DOE 数据对象头 2 中指示的 Length 长于特定数据对象所期望的长度，则数据对象中达到期望长度的部分必须被正常处理，其余部分必须被静默丢弃。
- 如果不支持该数据对象类型，则该数据对象必须被静默丢弃。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

The DOE Discovery feature must be implemented, and provides a means for software to discover the data object types supported by a DOE instance. The DOE Discovery type consists of the request and response data objects, with the 3rd DW of the data object content as defined in Table 6-30 and Table 6-31 respectively. Where indicated in Table 6-33, response data objects include a 4th DW, as defined in Table 6-32. The DOE Discovery data object feature must be operable in D0, D1, D2 and D3hot.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

必须实现 DOE Discovery 特性，它为软件提供发现某 DOE 实例所支持的数据对象类型的方法。DOE Discovery 类型由请求和响应数据对象组成，其数据对象内容的第 3 个 DW 分别如表 6-30 和表 6-31 所定义。在表 6-33 中指明的情况下，响应数据对象包含第 4 个 DW，如表 6-32 所定义。DOE Discovery 数据对象特性必须在 D0、D1、D2 和 D3hot 下可操作。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


> **Figure 6-43.** DOE Discovery Request Data Object Contents (3rd DW)
> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0890_1_tight.png" width="700">

**Table 6-30 DOE Discovery Request Data Object Contents (3rd DW)**

| Bit Location | Description |
|--------------|-------------|
| 7:0 | Index – Indicates DOE Discovery entry index queried. Indices must start at 00h and increase monotonically by 1. |
| 15:8 | DOE Discovery Version – must be 02h if the Capability Version in the Data Object Exchange Extended Capability is 02h or greater. |

**表 6-30 DOE Discovery 请求数据对象内容 (第 3 个 DW)**

| 位位置 (Bit Location) | 描述 (Description) |
|--------------|-------------|
| 7:0 | Index — 指示所查询的 DOE Discovery 条目索引。索引必须从 00h 起始，并以 1 为步长单调递增。 |
| 15:8 | DOE Discovery Version — 若 Data Object Exchange 扩展能力中的 Capability Version 为 02h 或更大，则必须为 02h。 |

<a id="sec-6-30-1-1"></a>
## 6.30.1.1 DOE Discovery Feature | DOE 发现功能

table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

> **Figure 6-44.** DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents (3rd DW)
> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0891_1_tight.png" width="700">

**Table 6-31. DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents (3rd DW) | 表 6-31. DOE 发现响应数据对象内容（第 3 个 DW）**

| Bit Location | Description |
|--------------|-------------|
| 31:16 | Reserved - Requesters must place 0000h in this field. Responders must ignore the value in this field. |
| 15:0 | Vendor ID – PCI-SIG Vendor ID of the entity that defined the type of data object. FFFFh if index is invalid or out of range. |
| 23:16 | Data Object Type – Indicates the identity of the data object type associated with the Index value supplied with the DOE Discovery Request.<br>The PCI-SIG defined data object type for DOE Discovery must be implemented at index 00h.<br>The index values used for other data object types is implementation specific and has no meaning defined by this specification.<br>Undefined if Vendor ID value is FFFFh. |
| 31:24 | Next Index – Indicates the next DOE Discovery Index value. If the responding DOE instance supports entries with indices greater than the index indicated in the received DOE Discovery Request, it must increment the queried index by 1 and return the resulting value in this field.<br>Must be 00h to indicate the final entry.<br>Undefined if Vendor ID value is FFFFh. |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**Table 6-31. DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents (3rd DW) | 表 6-31. DOE 发现响应数据对象内容（第 3 个 DW）**

| 位位置 | 描述 |
|--------------|-------------|
| 31:16 | 保留 - 请求者必须在本字段放置 0000h。响应者必须忽略本字段的值。 |
| 15:0 | Vendor ID（供应商 ID） – 定义该数据对象类型的实体的 PCI-SIG 供应商 ID。如果索引无效或超出范围，则为 FFFFh。 |
| 23:16 | Data Object Type（数据对象类型） – 标识与 DOE 发现请求中所提供 Index 值相关联的数据对象类型的身份。<br>PCI-SIG 定义的 DOE Discovery 数据对象类型必须在索引 00h 处实现。<br>用于其他数据对象类型的索引值是实现特定的，本规范未定义其含义。<br>当 Vendor ID 值为 FFFFh 时未定义。 |
| 31:24 | Next Index（下一索引） – 指示下一个 DOE Discovery 索引值。如果响应的 DOE 实例支持索引大于所接收到的 DOE Discovery Request 中所指示索引的条目，则必须将所查询的索引加 1 并将结果值返回到本字段中。<br>必须为 00h 以指示最后一个条目。<br>当 Vendor ID 值为 FFFFh 时未定义。 |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-6-30-1-1-4dw"></a>
## 6.30.1.1 DOE Discovery Feature (4th DW) | DOE 发现功能（第 4 个 DW）


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 6-32. DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents (4th DW) | 表 6-32. DOE 发现响应数据对象内容（第 4 个 DW）**

| Bit Location | Description |
|--------------|-------------|
| 17:0 | Max DO Length – Maximum length supported for this data object type, expressed as a number of DWs including the 2 DWs of the Data Object Header, encoded such that a value of 0 0002h indicates 2 DWs, and a value of 0 0000h indicates 2^18 DWs. A value of 0 0001h is not permitted.<br>Undefined if Vendor ID value is FFFFh. |
| 31:18 | Additional Info – The contents of this field are Data Object Type specific. Values are defined for each Data Object Type in § Table 6-33.<br>Undefined if Vendor ID value is FFFFh. |

PCI-SIG defined data object types are defined in § Table 6-33.

**Table 6-33. PCI-SIG Defined Data Object Types (Vendor ID = 0001h) | 表 6-33. PCI-SIG 定义的数据对象类型（Vendor ID = 0001h）**

| Vendor ID (h) | Data Object Type (h) | Description |
|---------------|----------------------|-------------|
| 0001 | 00 | **DOE Discovery** – Every DOE instance must support this data object feature and associated type, both indicated by this entry.<br>A requester uses DOE Discovery to discover all other Data Object types supported by the DOE instance.<br>Must not include the DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents (4th DW). |
| 0001 | 01 | **CMA-SPDM** – Data object type for SPDM messages. See § Section 6.31.<br>Permitted to include the DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents (4th DW). The Additional Info field is Reserved. |
| 0001 | 02 | **Secured CMA-SPDM** – Data object type for SPDM secure sessions. See § Section 6.31.4.<br>If this type is supported, then the CMA/SPDM (01h) type must also be supported.<br>Permitted to include the DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents (4th DW). The Additional Info field is Reserved. |
| 0001 | 03 | **CMA/SPDM with Connection ID** – Data object type for SPDM messages with Connection ID.<br>If this type is supported, then the CMA/SPDM (01h) type must also be supported.<br>Required to include the DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents 4th DW. Bits 11:0 of the Additional Info field must indicate the largest supported Connection ID value, such that all Connection IDs must be in the range from 0 to the indicated value:<br>0000 0000 0000b = 1 Connection ID<br>…<br>1111 1111 1111b = 4096 Connection IDs<br>Bits 13:12 of the Additional Info field are Reserved. |
| 0001 | 04 | **Secured CMA/SPDM with Connection ID** – Data object type for SPDM secure sessions with Connection ID.<br>If this type is supported, then the CMA/SPDM (01h) and CMA/SPDM with Connection ID (03h) types must also be supported.<br>Required to include the DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents 4th DW. Bits 11:0 of the Additional Info field must indicate the largest supported Connection ID value, such that all Connection IDs must be in the range from 0 to the indicated value:<br>0000 0000 0000b = 1 Connection ID<br>…<br>1111 1111 1111b = 4096 Connection IDs<br>Bits 13:12 of the Additional Info field are Reserved. |
| 0001 | 05 | **Async Message** – Data object type for messages generated asynchronously by the device.<br>Required to include the DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents 4th DW. |
| 0001 | 06 to FF | Reserved |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**Table 6-32. DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents (4th DW) | 表 6-32. DOE 发现响应数据对象内容（第 4 个 DW）**

| 位位置 | 描述 |
|--------------|-------------|
| 17:0 | Max DO Length（最大数据对象长度） – 该数据对象类型所支持的最大长度，以 DW 数量表示（包括数据对象头部的 2 个 DW），其编码方式为：值 0 0002h 表示 2 个 DW，值 0 0000h 表示 2^18 个 DW。值 0 0001h 不允许使用。<br>当 Vendor ID 值为 FFFFh 时未定义。 |
| 31:18 | Additional Info（附加信息） – 本字段的内容因数据对象类型而异。各数据对象类型的值在 § Table 6-33 中定义。<br>当 Vendor ID 值为 FFFFh 时未定义。 |

PCI-SIG 定义的数据对象类型在 § Table 6-33 中定义。

**Table 6-33. PCI-SIG 定义的数据对象类型（Vendor ID = 0001h） | 表 6-33. PCI-SIG 定义的数据对象类型（Vendor ID = 0001h）**

| Vendor ID (h) | Data Object Type (h) | 描述 |
|---------------|----------------------|-------------|
| 0001 | 00 | **DOE Discovery** – 每个 DOE 实例必须支持此数据对象功能及相关类型，两者均由本条目指示。<br>请求者使用 DOE Discovery 来发现 DOE 实例所支持的所有其他数据对象类型。<br>不得包含 DOE Discovery 响应数据对象内容（第 4 个 DW）。 |
| 0001 | 01 | **CMA-SPDM** – 用于 SPDM 消息的数据对象类型。参见 § Section 6.31。<br>允许包含 DOE Discovery 响应数据对象内容（第 4 个 DW）。Additional Info 字段为保留。 |
| 0001 | 02 | **Secured CMA-SPDM（安全 CMA-SPDM）** – 用于 SPDM 安全会话的数据对象类型。参见 § Section 6.31.4。<br>如果支持此类型，则必须同时支持 CMA/SPDM (01h) 类型。<br>允许包含 DOE Discovery 响应数据对象内容（第 4 个 DW）。Additional Info 字段为保留。 |
| 0001 | 03 | **CMA/SPDM with Connection ID（带 Connection ID 的 CMA/SPDM）** – 用于带 Connection ID 的 SPDM 消息的数据对象类型。<br>如果支持此类型，则必须同时支持 CMA/SPDM (01h) 类型。<br>必须包含 DOE Discovery 响应数据对象内容（第 4 个 DW）。Additional Info 字段的位 11:0 必须指示所支持的最大 Connection ID 值，所有 Connection ID 必须位于从 0 到所示值的范围内：<br>0000 0000 0000b = 1 个 Connection ID<br>…<br>1111 1111 1111b = 4096 个 Connection ID<br>Additional Info 字段的位 13:12 为保留。 |
| 0001 | 04 | **Secured CMA/SPDM with Connection ID（带 Connection ID 的安全 CMA/SPDM）** – 用于带 Connection ID 的 SPDM 安全会话的数据对象类型。<br>如果支持此类型，则必须同时支持 CMA/SPDM (01h) 和 CMA/SPDM with Connection ID (03h) 类型。<br>必须包含 DOE Discovery 响应数据对象内容（第 4 个 DW）。Additional Info 字段的位 11:0 必须指示所支持的最大 Connection ID 值，所有 Connection ID 必须位于从 0 到所示值的范围内：<br>0000 0000 0000b = 1 个 Connection ID<br>…<br>1111 1111 1111b = 4096 个 Connection ID<br>Additional Info 字段的位 13:12 为保留。 |
| 0001 | 05 | **Async Message（异步消息）** – 用于设备异步生成的消息的数据对象类型。<br>必须包含 DOE Discovery 响应数据对象内容（第 4 个 DW）。 |
| 0001 | 06 至 FF | 保留 |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 6-45.** DOE Discovery Response Data Object Contents (4th DW)
> <img src="figures/chapter_06/fig_0892_1.png" width="700">
